title: crataegus alleghaniensis var. extraria
intro: Crataegus alleghaniensis var. extraria, also known as the Allegheny hawthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to eastern North America, characterized by its thorny branches and small white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardiovascular benefits. The health benefits of Crataegus alleghaniensis var. extraria include reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and protecting against oxidative stress. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be used to support heart health and overall well-being.
